# License guidance

Every public repository must have an explicit, company-approved license. Do not add a license based only on convenience.

- Use a proprietary notice for source that is not intentionally open-sourced.
- Consider Apache-2.0 when an approved open-source project benefits from explicit patent terms.
- Consider MIT for approved small libraries or examples when minimal terms are appropriate.
- Review third-party assets, model weights, datasets and generated code separately.
- Record copyright ownership and obtain legal approval before publication.
